cards.back: IC = (VCC − VCE)/(RC + RE). Straight line on IC vs VCE. x-int: VCC. y-int: VCC/(RC+RE).
VCE = VCC (when IC = 0). All supply voltage across the transistor.
IC = VCC/(RC + RE) (when VCE = 0). Maximum possible DC current.
Slope = −1/RAC where RAC = RC || RL. Steeper than DC line because RAC < RC+RE.
VCE = VCEQ + ICQ × RAC. This is the maximum VCE during AC swing.
1. Find IB swing from input. 2. Mark IB max/min on output chars. 3. Read VCE at AC load line intersections. 4. Plot VCE(t).
VCE drops to ≈0.2V. Output waveform is flattened at the bottom. Can't go lower.
IC drops to 0. VCE rises to VCEQ + ICQ·RAC. Output flattened at the top.

cards.front: Why is AC load line for AC, not DC?
cards.back: AC: caps short (RE bypassed, RL connected). Different constraints than DC (caps open).
The Q-point (VCEQ, ICQ). It's the DC operating point and the centre of AC swing.
